about-me.name: About
about-me.presentation: Hello 👋 I'm a full-stack developer specializing in JavaScript, with a strong focus on React and NestJS. After training in embedded systems, I decided to specialize in web development, a field where I truly thrive. I've had the opportunity to work on a wide variety of projects: web and mobile applications, automation systems, monitoring tools, indie video games... My experience at Université Côte d'Azur allowed me to grow in an international, innovative, and open-source environment. I enjoy designing useful and efficient interfaces, contributing to the deployment of applications, and continuously improving existing systems. Curious, rigorous, and passionate, I'm always looking to learn more and get involved in new projects.

overcraft.name: Overcraft
overcraft.description: Overcraft is a platform where StarCraft 2 players can create, organize, and share their build orders to better learn and optimize their strategies. It features an interactive mode with a timer for real-time execution of builds, along with customization and editing options.

web-cv.name: Web CV
web-cv.description: This website combines my resume and portfolio in an interactive way, providing a better showcase of my skills, achievements, and technical projects.

island-ship.name: Island Ship
island-ship.description: A tower defense game developed with Godot in C#, this project marked my first steps into game development. It allowed me to experiment with enemy management, attack waves, defense logic, and real-time gameplay mechanics.

ulysseus-toolbox.name: Ulysseus Toolbox
ulysseus-toolbox.description: I developed an intuitive web interface that allows non-technical members of the Ulysseus team to run automated scripts without technical intervention. This solution notably simplifies the synchronization of Microsoft Azure distribution lists from an Excel file hosted on SharePoint. The project was approved right after the prototype presentation, integrated via a fork into the private GitLab, and then deployed to production.

universite-cote-d-azur-ulysseus.name: Université Côte d'Azur - Ulysseus
universite-cote-d-azur-ulysseus.description: System updates, application development and maintenance for the Ulysseus alliance of institutions.

match-4-cooperation.name: Match4Cooperation
match-4-cooperation.description: Match4Cooperation (M4C) is a social network developed by the Ulysseus European University Alliance to connect researchers and academic staff, foster collaboration, and increase visibility of European research funding opportunities. Accessible via mobile app or web version, M4C allows Ulysseus members to log in using their university credentials, while guests can join by invitation. The platform facilitates interaction, tracking of research activity, and partner search via the dedicated @projects group. M4C supports researcher mobility and strengthens a collaborative academic community within the Ulysseus alliance.

developed-features.1: Redesigned the group creation system using the Mastodon API, reducing creation time from over an hour to just a few seconds by implementing a status tracking system that also informs the user of progress.
developed-features.2: Used the Mastodon API to display followers and followings on the user profile.
developed-features.4: Enhanced the overall UI of the application.
developed-features.5: Published the app on the Google and Apple stores.

open-science-repository.name: Open Science Repository
open-science-repository.description: The Ulysseus Open Science Repository is a gateway for exploring and contributing to open access research within the Ulysseus alliance. It is designed to make science produced by Ulysseus accessible to all, enabling users to search for publications and discover innovative work across the alliance.

developed-features.1: Redesigned the homepage displaying Ulysseus alliance universities, with a button redirecting to the publications of the selected university's researchers.
developed-features.2: Improved native search (initially limited to publications) to now also show profiles and organizations containing references to the entered term.
developed-features.3: Merged an external application (form and researcher expertise data) and integrated it into InvenioRDM.

1.name: InvenioRDM Partners Meeting, March 2024
1.description: I attended the annual InvenioRDM community meeting in Münster, Germany, from March 18–22, 2024. The event gathered 46 participants from over fifteen institutions to collaborate and work on InvenioRDM development. Using the Open Space Technology method, participants proposed and chose discussion topics. We covered topics such as pull request management, version 12 planning, and large file handling. It was a great opportunity for community collaboration and project advancement.

2.name: External App Integration
2.description: Due to budget constraints involving a third-party company, we took over an app originally developed by an external provider. We kept the existing database but completely rebuilt the application in Python and JavaScript, preserving the data while modernizing the system.

airflow.name: AirFlow
airflow.description: Development of an automated monitoring system (PINGER) using AirFlow to check the availability of our web applications and monitor the validity of SSL certificates across our domains.

developed-features.1: Created a `pinger` script running every 5 minutes to check service availability. If a service is down, an email is sent with the detected error.
developed-features.2: Developed a script to check SSL certificate validity and send reminders starting 15 days before expiration.

member-curriculum.name: Member Curriculum
member-curriculum.description: This project scrapes the websites of Ulysseus alliance institutions to collect available degree programs. The data is then centralized into a single training directory accessible to students. This system not only better informs students about opportunities within the alliance but also supports double-degree development by identifying similar programs in the same field across institutions (developed entirely from scratch: frontend, backend, and production deployment).

developed-features.1: Created and developed the Git repository for frontend, backend, and deployment components.
developed-features.2: Developed all parts of the project independently, including scraping data from institutional websites.
developed-features.3: Designed a training similarity system to suggest multiple programs related to a selected topic.
developed-features.5: Deployed and launched the application under a subroute of the Ulysseus domain.

1.name: Student Portal
1.description: This project will be discontinued and fully integrated into the student platform.

student-portal.name: Student Portal
student-portal.description: Development of a centralized student portal to display events and activities across campuses, improving information access and student participation. The portal includes features such as listing degree programs offered by Ulysseus, job offers from each institution and surrounding areas, a chat system, and a housing exchange platform to help students find accommodation during exchanges. The app focuses on supporting student mobility.

developed-features.1: Application developed as an extension of the Member Curriculum project.
developed-features.3: Deployed and launched the application under a subroute of the Ulysseus domain.

1.name: Member Curriculum
1.description: Student Portal is an evolution of this project.

microsoft-azure.name: Microsoft - Azure
microsoft-azure.description: Automated the process of adding new users to the Ulysseus tenant via a Python script, making access management and onboarding easier.

developed-features.1: Improved the user account creation script on Ulysseus' Microsoft Azure tenant.
developed-features.2: Created a script to synchronize distribution lists from an Excel file: adding/removing members and list owners.

1.name: Ulysseus Toolbox
1.description: This project inspired the idea to design the user interface for the Ulysseus Toolbox.

servers-details.name: Servers Details
servers-details.description: Automated the retrieval of detailed server information, including performance, status, and configuration data, with Excel report generation for easy tracking and analysis.

developed-features.1: Retrieved information for each server, including the installed Docker version and versions of used Docker images.

expert-database.name: Experts Database
expert-database.description: The Ulysseus Experts Database offers academic staff from partner universities the chance to connect and engage in high-impact research, teaching, and innovation initiatives. By completing the form, individuals are added to the database, allowing Ulysseus to reach out when their profile matches specific initiatives like EU project proposals, transnational research groups, teaching activities, conferences, fairs, workshops, or training programs.

developed-features.1: Production deployment of fixes made by the external company.

1.name: Open Science Repository
1.description: Experts Database will be discontinued and fully integrated into the Open Science Repository.

licence.name: Bachelor's Degree in Embedded Systems
licence.description: Programming languages: C / C++ and VHDL (PIC16, ARDUINO, STM32). Real-time operating systems with FreeRTOS. Linux and Windows operating systems.

bts.name: Higher Technical Diploma in Digital Systems
bts.description: Circuit diagram analysis, design and troubleshooting of electronic boards, signal analysis, Arduino programming, HTML/CSS.

contact-me.subject: Subject
